purpose: IPv6 Neighbor Discovery (ND) is the IPv6 replacement for IPv4 ARP plus ICMP router discovery. It resolves on-link IPv6 addresses to link-layer (MAC) addresses using Neighbor Solicitation/Advertisement (NS/NA), discovers routers and on-link prefixes via Router Solicitation/Advertisement (RS/RA), drives Stateless Address Autoconfiguration (SLAAC), runs Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) before an address is used, and continuously tracks neighbor reachability through Neighbor Unreachability Detection (NUD). All ND messages are ICMPv6 (types 133-137) and use the link-local source address, so a working link-local address and unfiltered ICMPv6 are prerequisites for any IPv6 forwarding on the segment.

states.name: INCOMPLETE
states.description: Address resolution is in progress: a Neighbor Solicitation has been multicast to the target's solicited-node multicast address, but no Neighbor Advertisement has been received yet. Packets to the neighbor are queued (a small number) and dropped if resolution never completes.

states.name: REACHABLE
states.description: Positive confirmation was received within the last ReachableTime milliseconds that the forward path to the neighbor is working. No probing is needed while in this state.

states.name: STALE
states.description: More than ReachableTime has elapsed since the last reachability confirmation. The cached link-layer address is still used to send, but reachability is unverified. STALE is normal and is NOT a failure.

states.name: DELAY
states.description: A packet was sent to a STALE neighbor; the node waits DELAY_FIRST_PROBE_TIME for an upper-layer reachability hint before actively probing. Transient state.

states.name: PROBE
states.description: Reachability is being actively verified by retransmitting unicast Neighbor Solicitations every RetransTimer. If MAX_UNICAST_SOLICIT probes go unanswered the entry is deleted and resolution restarts.

states.name: TENTATIVE
states.description: An address (link-local or SLAAC/configured) is undergoing Duplicate Address Detection. It is not yet assigned and cannot be used as a source/destination until DAD completes.

states.name: DUPLICATE
states.description: DAD detected another node defending the address (a NA or a competing NS was received for the tentative address). The address is NOT assigned; if it is the interface link-local, IPv6 operation on the interface is disabled (RFC 4862 5.4.5).

transitions.trigger: No cache entry exists (or it was flushed) and a packet must be sent: a multicast NS is issued to the solicited-node multicast address.
A solicited Neighbor Advertisement with the Solicited flag set is received.
After MAX_MULTICAST_SOLICIT (3) NS retransmissions with no NA the entry is discarded and packets to the neighbor are dropped (address unresolvable).
ReachableTime (default 30s) elapses since the last positive confirmation, or an unsolicited NA/NS with a different link-layer address is received.
A packet is sent to a neighbor whose entry is STALE.
DELAY_FIRST_PROBE_TIME (5s) elapses with no upper-layer reachability confirmation; unicast NS probing begins.
A solicited NA is received in response to a unicast probe.
MAX_UNICAST_SOLICIT (3) unicast probes go unanswered; the entry is deleted and the neighbor must be re-resolved from scratch.
DAD completes with no defending NA after DupAddrDetectTransmits probes; the address is assigned and usable.
A Neighbor Advertisement (or a simultaneous DAD NS) is received for the tentative address, proving another node owns it.

timers.name: ReachableTime
timers.description: Time a neighbor remains in REACHABLE after a confirmation before transitioning to STALE. Advertised to hosts in the Router Advertisement Reachable Time field.
timers.on_expiry: Neighbor cache entry moves REACHABLE -> STALE (still usable; not a failure).
timers.tuning_advice: Tens of seconds. Lower for faster failure detection at the cost of more NS probes.

os_defaults.frr_path: neighsyncd mirrors kernel NEIGH state into STATE_DB NEIGH_TABLE

timers.name: RetransTimer
timers.description: Interval between retransmitted Neighbor Solicitations while resolving (INCOMPLETE) or probing (PROBE). Advertised to hosts in the RA Retrans Timer field.
timers.on_expiry: Another NS is retransmitted; after MAX_*_SOLICIT attempts the entry is discarded.
timers.tuning_advice: 1-2 s typical. Too low floods NS; too high slows resolution.

timers.name: DELAY_FIRST_PROBE_TIME
timers.description: After sending to a STALE neighbor, how long the node waits in DELAY for an upper-layer reachability confirmation before unicast probing (NUD).
timers.on_expiry: Neighbor cache entry moves DELAY -> PROBE and unicast NS probing begins.

timers.name: MaxRtrAdvInterval
timers.description: Maximum interval between unsolicited multicast Router Advertisements on an advertising interface. Hosts that miss RAs lose default-gateway/prefix info.
timers.on_expiry: Next unsolicited RA is due; if RAs stop, host default route and SLAAC prefixes age out.
timers.tuning_advice: 200-600 s typical. Lower for faster host convergence; raises multicast RA load.

os_defaults.config_path: interface: ipv6 nd ra-interval <sec> (0 disables RA via suppress)

os_defaults.default: radvd/FRR ipv6 nd ra-interval (when acting as RA sender)
os_defaults.config_path: FRR: interface <ifname> / no ipv6 nd suppress-ra / ipv6 nd ra-interval <sec>

timers.name: DupAddrDetectTransmits
timers.description: Number of Neighbor Solicitations sent during Duplicate Address Detection before an address is considered unique and assignable. 0 disables DAD.
timers.on_expiry: If no defending NA arrives, the tentative address is assigned; if one does, the address is marked DUPLICATE and not assigned.
timers.tuning_advice: Keep >=1. Disabling DAD risks silent duplicate-address black holes.

messages.name: Router Solicitation (RS) — ICMPv6 type 133
messages.description: Prompts routers to send Router Advertisements immediately rather than waiting for the next periodic RA. Destination is ff02::2 (all-routers multicast).
messages.when_sent: Sent by a host, typically at interface bring-up / boot, to discover routers quickly.
key_fields: source: host link-local (or :: before address config)
option: Source Link-Layer Address (omitted when source is ::)

messages.name: Router Advertisement (RA) — ICMPv6 type 134
messages.description: Carries on-link prefix(es), router lifetime (default gateway), MTU, and the M/O flags that decide SLAAC vs DHCPv6. The basis of SLAAC and IPv6 default-gateway discovery.
messages.when_sent: Sent by routers periodically (every MaxRtrAdvInterval) and in response to an RS.
key_fields: Cur Hop Limit, Router Lifetime (0 = not a default router)
M flag (Managed: addresses via DHCPv6), O flag (Other config via DHCPv6)
Prefix Information option: prefix, L/A flags, valid & preferred lifetimes
MTU option, Source Link-Layer Address option, RDNSS (RFC 8106)

messages.name: Neighbor Solicitation (NS) — ICMPv6 type 135
messages.description: Resolves a neighbor's link-layer address (to the target's solicited-node multicast ff02::1:ffXX:XXXX), probes reachability (unicast, NUD), and performs DAD.
messages.when_sent: Sent to resolve an unknown neighbor, to probe a STALE/PROBE neighbor (unicast), or during DAD (source ::, target = tentative address).
key_fields: source: :: during DAD, otherwise the sender's address
Source Link-Layer Address option (present except during DAD)

messages.name: Neighbor Advertisement (NA) — ICMPv6 type 136
messages.description: Carries the Router (R), Solicited (S) and Override (O) flags plus the Target Link-Layer Address; the response that resolves or confirms a neighbor.
messages.when_sent: Sent in response to an NS, or unsolicited (multicast) when a node's link-layer address changes.
key_fields: R flag (sender is a router), S flag (solicited response), O flag (override cache)
Target Link-Layer Address option (the resolved MAC)

messages.name: Redirect — ICMPv6 type 137
messages.description: Informs a host of a better first-hop for a destination (a more specific on-link neighbor or a different router).
messages.when_sent: Sent by a router when it forwards a packet out the same interface it arrived on and a better first-hop exists.
key_fields: Target Address (better first-hop), Destination Address being redirected
optional Redirected Header / Target Link-Layer Address options

dependencies.name: ICMPv6
dependencies.description: All ND messages (types 133-137) are ICMPv6. An ACL or control-plane policy that drops ICMPv6 breaks ND entirely — not just ping. ICMPv6 Packet Too Big (type 2) is also required for IPv6 PMTUD.

dependencies.name: Link-local address
dependencies.description: Every IPv6 interface needs a link-local (fe80::/10) address. ND uses it as the source of NS/NA/RS and routers source RAs from it. Without a valid link-local, ND and all IPv6 routing adjacencies (OSPFv3, BGP over fe80::) fail.

dependencies.name: Solicited-node multicast / MLD
dependencies.description: NS for resolution is sent to the target's solicited-node multicast group (ff02::1:ffXX:XXXX). Nodes must join it (MLD) and the L2 fabric must deliver the multicast; MLD-snooping misconfiguration can starve ND.

dependencies.name: Control-plane policing (CoPP)
dependencies.description: ND packets are punted to the CPU. Aggressive CoPP or an ND scan can rate-limit/drop NS/NA, causing intermittent resolution failures under load.

dependencies.name: neighsyncd (SONiC)
dependencies.description: In SONiC, the Linux kernel runs ND; neighsyncd mirrors kernel NEIGH entries into STATE_DB NEIGH_TABLE, and orchagent programs the resolved neighbor as a SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_NEIGHBOR_ENTRY in ASIC_DB for hardware forwarding.

failure_modes.scenario: IPv6 neighbor stuck INCOMPLETE — ND not resolving the next-hop/host
failure_modes.description: A neighbor entry never leaves INCOMPLETE: NS is multicast to the solicited-node address but no NA returns, so packets to that IPv6 are dropped. Causes mirror IPv4 'incomplete ARP' (host down, wrong VLAN, L2 break) plus IPv6-specific ones: ICMPv6/NS filtered by an ACL, the node never joined the solicited-node multicast group, or MLD-snooping is pruning the NS.
symptoms: show ipv6 neighbor shows the address in INCOMPLETE state with no MAC
Ping to the IPv6 next-hop/host fails while the interface is up
Traffic to destinations via that next-hop is black-holed
NS transmit counters increment with no matching NA received

root_causes.cause: Target host is down, or its IPv6 stack/interface is not up to answer NS
Neighbor is on a different VLAN/SVI than the one ND is sent on (L2/L3 mismatch)
An ACL or CoPP policy is dropping ICMPv6 NS/NA (types 135/136) on the path
Solicited-node multicast (ff02::1:ffXX:XXXX) not delivered — MLD-snooping pruning or the node never joined the group
Wrong/duplicate link-layer (MAC) mapping, or the neighbor uses a different link-local than expected
sonic: kernel resolved the neighbor but orchagent failed to program the SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_NEIGHBOR_ENTRY into ASIC_DB

resolutions.action: Confirm the neighbor is reachable at L2: verify it is up and on the same VLAN/segment
Confirm the neighbor is reachable at L2: verify it is up and on the same VLAN/segment
Confirm the neighbor is reachable at L2: verify it is up and on the same VLAN/segment
Check that ICMPv6 NS/NA (types 135/136) and RS/RA (133/134) are permitted by any interface/CoPP ACL on the path
Check that ICMPv6 NS/NA (types 135/136) and RS/RA (133/134) are permitted by any interface/CoPP ACL on the path
Check that ICMPv6 NS/NA (types 135/136) and RS/RA (133/134) are permitted by any interface/CoPP ACL on the path
Ping the neighbor to force re-resolution and watch the cache transition out of INCOMPLETE
Ping the neighbor to force re-resolution and watch the cache transition out of INCOMPLETE
Ping the neighbor to force re-resolution and watch the cache transition out of INCOMPLETE

sonic: verify kernel resolution then ASIC programming: 'show ndp <ip>' -> 'redis-cli -n 6 HGETALL "NEIGH_TABLE|<ifname>|<ip>"' -> 'redis-cli -n 1 KEYS "ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_NEIGHBOR_ENTRY*" | grep <ip>'
sonic: if kernel has the neighbor but ASIC_DB does not, check 'docker logs swss' for SAI neighbor errors; restarting swss forces a resync (disruptive)

failure_modes.scenario: Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) failure silently unconfigures the IPv6 address
failure_modes.description: When an address is configured (link-local, SLAAC, or static), the node runs DAD by soliciting the tentative address. If a defending NA (or competing NS) is received, the address is DUPLICATE and is NOT assigned. If the duplicate is the interface link-local, RFC 4862 5.4.5 requires IPv6 operation on the interface to be disabled — so IPv6 silently fails to come up with little more than a log line. Common with duplicate static addresses, a looped segment reflecting the node's own NS, or duplicate virtual MACs (HSRPv6/VRRPv3).
symptoms: Configured IPv6 address is missing from the interface despite being in config
Interface shows the address as DUPLICATE / DAD failed / TENTATIVE-stuck
%IPV6 / DAD duplicate-address syslog for the address
IPv6 does not come up on the interface at all when the link-local is the duplicate

root_causes.cause: Another node on the link is genuinely configured with the same IPv6 address
A L2 loop (or hairpin) reflects the node's own DAD NS back, so it appears defended
Duplicate virtual MAC across redundancy groups (HSRPv6/VRRPv3) generating the same EUI-64 / link-local
Re-used static address after a device swap while the old neighbor is still online

resolutions.action: Identify the address state and the conflicting node, then change one address or remove the duplicate
Identify the address state and the conflicting node, then change one address or remove the duplicate
Identify the address state and the conflicting node, then change one address or remove the duplicate
Check for an L2 loop reflecting DAD NS (STP topology, dual-homing) before assuming a real duplicate
Check for an L2 loop reflecting DAD NS (STP topology, dual-homing) before assuming a real duplicate
Check for an L2 loop reflecting DAD NS (STP topology, dual-homing) before assuming a real duplicate
After resolving the conflict, bounce the interface (shut/no shut) or reconfigure the address to re-run DAD
After resolving the conflict, bounce the interface (shut/no shut) or reconfigure the address to re-run DAD
After resolving the conflict, bounce the interface (shut/no shut) or reconfigure the address to re-run DAD
sonic: check 'show ipv6 interfaces' for dadfailed/tentative, inspect 'ip -6 address show dev <ifname>' for 'dadfailed', then correct the duplicate and 'config interface ip remove/add' to re-run DAD

verify_commands.look_for: address state: TENTATIVE/DUPLICATE vs valid; DAD attempts

failure_modes.scenario: Missing or failed link-local address breaks ND and all IPv6 forwarding on the link
failure_modes.description: ND sources NS/NA/RS from the interface link-local (fe80::/10) and routers source RAs from it; routing protocols use link-local next-hops. If the link-local is absent (IPv6 not enabled on the interface) or itself failed DAD, no ND happens: neighbors never resolve, RAs are neither sent nor accepted, and OSPFv3/BGP-over-link-local adjacencies never form — even though global addresses look configured.
symptoms: No fe80:: address on the interface in show ipv6 interface
All neighbors on the segment stuck INCOMPLETE despite correct global addressing
OSPFv3 / IS-IS / BGP sessions that rely on link-local next-hops never come up
Router neither sends nor is seen in host 'show ipv6 routers'

root_causes.cause: IPv6 not enabled on the interface (no 'ipv6 enable' / no global address to auto-derive a link-local)
The link-local itself failed DAD (duplicate fe80::), disabling IPv6 on the interface
Interface is L2/down or in the wrong VRF so the link-local is not installed
sonic: interface has no IPv6 enabled / 'ipv6 link-local' mode not set

resolutions.action: Enable IPv6 on the interface so a link-local is derived ('ipv6 enable' or configure a global address)
Enable IPv6 on the interface so a link-local is derived ('ipv6 enable' or configure a global address)
Enable IPv6 on the interface so a link-local is derived ('ipv6 enable' or configure a global address)
If the link-local failed DAD, resolve the fe80:: duplicate (often a duplicate MAC) then re-enable the interface
If the link-local failed DAD, resolve the fe80:: duplicate (often a duplicate MAC) then re-enable the interface
If the link-local failed DAD, resolve the fe80:: duplicate (often a duplicate MAC) then re-enable the interface
sonic: verify IPv6/link-local with 'show ipv6 interfaces' and 'show ipv6 link-local-mode'; enable link-local mode if required for the interface

verify_commands.confirms_if: Absent link-local confirms ND cannot operate
verify_commands.look_for: a fe80:: link-local listed as valid (not TENTATIVE/DUPLICATE)

verify_commands.confirms_if: Absent link-local confirms ND cannot operate
verify_commands.look_for: 'IPv6 is enabled, link-local address is fe80::...'

verify_commands.confirms_if: Absent link-local confirms ND cannot operate
verify_commands.look_for: link-local address line present and valid

verify_commands.confirms_if: Missing link-local confirms IPv6 not operational on the port
verify_commands.look_for: fe80:: address on the interface

verify_commands.confirms_if: Confirms whether link-local-only mode is enabled where needed
verify_commands.look_for: per-interface link-local mode state

failure_modes.scenario: Rogue or unexpected Router Advertisement hijacks the IPv6 default gateway / prefix
failure_modes.description: Any node on the link can send RAs. A rogue RA (a misconfigured host sharing its connection, a stray router, or an attacker) makes hosts install a wrong default gateway and SLAAC a wrong prefix, causing black-holing or interception. The fix is RA Guard on access ports plus controlling who may advertise.
symptoms: Hosts intermittently get a default gateway / prefix from an unexpected router
Two default routers appear in host 'show ipv6 routers' / 'ip -6 route'
IPv6 connectivity flaps as hosts oscillate between the real and rogue RA
Unexpected source link-local listed as a router on the segment

root_causes.cause: A host with IPv6 forwarding/ICS enabled is emitting RAs
An unauthorized or misconfigured router on the access VLAN is advertising
No RA Guard / ND inspection on access ports to drop RAs from non-router ports
Deliberate RA spoofing (man-in-the-middle) on an unsecured segment

resolutions.action: Identify the rogue RA source link-local and locate the port/MAC, then shut or remediate it
Identify the rogue RA source link-local and locate the port/MAC, then shut or remediate it
Identify the rogue RA source link-local and locate the port/MAC, then shut or remediate it
Enable RA Guard on access ports so only authorized router ports may send RAs (nxos/iosxe: 'ipv6 nd raguard')
Enable RA Guard on access ports so only authorized router ports may send RAs (nxos/iosxe: 'ipv6 nd raguard')
iosxr: apply ICMPv6 RA filtering / use a router-port policy to block RAs from host ports
sonic: filter inbound ICMPv6 type 134 (RA) on host-facing ports via ACL, allowing it only on uplinks to legitimate routers

verify_commands.confirms_if: More than one router / an unexpected source confirms a rogue RA
verify_commands.look_for: router link-local addresses heard and their advertised lifetime/prefix

verify_commands.confirms_if: Confirms whether RA Guard policy exists to block rogue RAs
verify_commands.look_for: RA Guard policy and where it is applied

verify_commands.confirms_if: Unexpected router entry confirms a rogue RA on the segment
verify_commands.look_for: all routers heard; compare against the known-good gateway

verify_commands.confirms_if: Confirms RA Guard configuration
verify_commands.look_for: configured raguard policies and bindings

verify_commands.confirms_if: Shows RA send/receive state to spot unexpected advertisers
verify_commands.look_for: RA parameters and any anomalous router

verify_commands.confirms_if: Multiple default routes with different next-hops confirm a rogue RA
verify_commands.look_for: default via fe80::... entries; an unexpected next-hop link-local

failure_modes.scenario: Router not advertising RA / prefix — hosts get no SLAAC address or default gateway
failure_modes.description: Hosts depend on RAs to learn the default gateway (non-zero Router Lifetime) and the on-link prefix with the Autonomous (A) flag for SLAAC. If RAs are suppressed, the router lifetime is 0, or the prefix lacks the A flag, hosts get a link-local only, no default route, and no global SLAAC address.
symptoms: Hosts have only a link-local (fe80::) and no global IPv6 address
Hosts have no IPv6 default route despite a working router on the link
show ipv6 routers on a host is empty
SLAAC works for some prefixes but not others (A-flag missing on one)

root_causes.cause: RA suppressed on the interface ('ipv6 nd suppress-ra' / FRR 'ipv6 nd suppress-ra' default)
Router Lifetime advertised as 0, so the router is not used as a default gateway
Prefix advertised without the Autonomous (A) flag, so hosts will not SLAAC from it
M flag set (managed) with no working DHCPv6, so hosts wait for DHCPv6 that never answers
sonic/FRR: 'no ipv6 nd suppress-ra' was never configured on the interface

resolutions.action: Confirm RA is enabled (not suppressed) on the downstream interface
Confirm RA is enabled (not suppressed) on the downstream interface
Confirm RA is enabled (not suppressed) on the downstream interface
Ensure a non-zero RA lifetime and that the on-link prefix carries the Autonomous flag for SLAAC
Ensure a non-zero RA lifetime and that the on-link prefix carries the Autonomous flag for SLAAC
Ensure a non-zero RA lifetime and that the on-link prefix carries the Autonomous flag for SLAAC
If M=1 is intended, verify DHCPv6 server/relay is reachable; otherwise clear M to allow SLAAC
If M=1 is intended, verify DHCPv6 server/relay is reachable; otherwise clear M to allow SLAAC
If M=1 is intended, verify DHCPv6 server/relay is reachable; otherwise clear M to allow SLAAC
sonic/FRR: enable RA on the interface: 'interface <ifname>' / 'no ipv6 nd suppress-ra' and set 'ipv6 nd ra-interval'/prefix as needed

verify_commands.look_for: RA suppress, lifetime, prefix A/L flags

verify_commands.look_for: RA enabled/suppressed, lifetime, prefix flags

verify_commands.look_for: interface listed as advertising with interval/lifetime

failure_modes.scenario: ND cache exhaustion / neighbor scan — INCOMPLETE entries flood the control plane
failure_modes.description: The huge IPv6 host space means scanning a /64 (or a misrouted prefix) makes the router create an INCOMPLETE entry and emit NS for every probed address. This fills the ND cache, saturates CoPP, and starves legitimate resolution — the IPv6 analogue of the IPv4 interface-static proxy-ARP storm.
symptoms: ND cache near or at platform capacity with thousands of INCOMPLETE entries
High control-plane CPU and NS transmit rate
Legitimate neighbors intermittently fail to resolve under the load
CoPP drop counters for ICMPv6/ND climbing

root_causes.cause: External scan or misconfiguration directing traffic to many unused addresses in an on-link /64
A glue/static route pointing at a large on-link prefix forces ND for every destination
No ND cache limit / unreachable-entry throttling configured for the interface
CoPP class for ICMPv6/ND too permissive, letting the scan reach the CPU unbounded

resolutions.action: Identify the scanned prefix and source, then filter or rate-limit it upstream
Identify the scanned prefix and source, then filter or rate-limit it upstream
Identify the scanned prefix and source, then filter or rate-limit it upstream
Apply an ND cache limit / destination-guard and tune CoPP for ICMPv6 to bound NS generation
Apply an ND cache limit / destination-guard and tune CoPP for ICMPv6 to bound NS generation
Apply an ND cache limit / destination-guard and tune CoPP for ICMPv6 to bound NS generation
Prefer routed (off-link) addressing over a giant on-link /64 where many addresses are unused
Prefer routed (off-link) addressing over a giant on-link /64 where many addresses are unused
Prefer routed (off-link) addressing over a giant on-link /64 where many addresses are unused
sonic: rate-limit ICMPv6/ND to the CPU via CoPP/control-plane ACL and watch STATE_DB NEIGH_TABLE growth

verify_commands.look_for: total vs INCOMPLETE entry counts

verify_commands.confirms_if: Elevated NS/ND rate confirms the storm
verify_commands.look_for: ICMPv6/ND message rates on the interface

verify_commands.confirms_if: High NS-sent with low NA confirms scan-driven resolution
verify_commands.look_for: NS sent vs NA received counts

verify_commands.confirms_if: Per-interface ND state confirms which interface is flooded
verify_commands.look_for: ND interface entries and counts

verify_commands.confirms_if: ND operational counters confirm the surge
verify_commands.look_for: NS/NA counters and cache size

verify_commands.confirms_if: Large neighbor-table count confirms exhaustion
verify_commands.look_for: number of NEIGH_TABLE entries vs platform norm

failure_modes.scenario: ICMPv6 filtered by ACL breaks ND (and PMTUD) — not just ping
failure_modes.description: Operators often port IPv4 ACLs that 'deny icmp' to IPv6, not realizing ND itself is ICMPv6. Filtering ICMPv6 types 133-136 kills neighbor and router discovery; filtering type 2 (Packet Too Big) silently breaks IPv6 Path MTU Discovery. The result looks like a routing or link problem but is an ACL problem.
symptoms: Neighbors stop resolving (INCOMPLETE) right after an ACL change
Hosts lose their default gateway/prefix because RS/RA are dropped
Large flows hang while small packets pass (Packet Too Big filtered -> PMTUD black hole)
Connectivity returns when the ICMPv6 ACL is removed

root_causes.cause: ACL denies ICMPv6 broadly, dropping ND types 133-136
ACL drops ICMPv6 type 2 (Packet Too Big), breaking IPv6 PMTUD
Infrastructure/uRPF/CoPP policy not permitting link-local-sourced ND
Reflexive/stateful rule not accounting for multicast-destined NS (solicited-node)

notes.note: Neighbor cache: 'show ipv6 neighbor [detail]'. RA control per interface with 'ipv6 nd ra-interval/ra-lifetime/suppress-ra'; RA Guard via 'ipv6 nd raguard'. DAD attempts via 'ipv6 nd dad attempts'. In VXLAN/EVPN, ND suppression and the distributed anycast gateway change where NS/NA are answered.

Neighbor cache: 'show ipv6 neighbors'. 'show ipv6 interface <intf>' shows the link-local, DAD attempts, and RA parameters; '[DUP]' marks a DAD-failed address. RA Guard and IPv6 Source Guard/device-tracking ('show device-tracking database') secure ND on access ports. RA interval default is 200 s (not the RFC 600 s).

Rich ND tooling: 'show ipv6 nd', 'show ipv6 nd dadfail', 'show ipv6 nd slaac location', 'show ipv6 nd packets', 'show ipv6 neighbors'. ND runs per-line-card; use the 'location' keyword to scope to a node when resolving distributed-platform issues.

The Linux kernel performs ND; 'show ndp' renders the kernel neighbor cache and 'ip -6 neighbor show' is the raw view. neighsyncd mirrors kernel NEIGH into STATE_DB NEIGH_TABLE and orchagent programs SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_NEIGHBOR_ENTRY into ASIC_DB for hardware forwarding. When SONiC acts as an RA sender, FRR zebra rtadv drives RAs ('no ipv6 nd suppress-ra').

sources.title: RFC 4861 — Neighbor Discovery for IP version 6 (IPv6) [Narten et al., 2007]
sources.verifying_quote: This document specifies the Neighbor Discovery protocol for IP Version 6. IPv6 nodes on the same link use Neighbor Discovery to discover each other's presence, to determine each other's link-layer addresses, to find routers, and to maintain reachability information about the paths to active neighbors.

sources.title: RFC 4861 Section 7.3.2 / Section 10 — Neighbor Cache states and protocol constants
sources.verifying_quote: A Neighbor Cache entry can be in one of five states: INCOMPLETE, REACHABLE, STALE, DELAY, PROBE. (REACHABLE_TIME 30,000 ms; RETRANS_TIMER 1,000 ms; DELAY_FIRST_PROBE_TIME 5 seconds.)

sources.title: RFC 4862 — IPv6 Stateless Address Autoconfiguration (SLAAC / DAD) [Thomson et al., 2007]
sources.verifying_quote: Duplicate Address Detection MUST be performed on all unicast addresses prior to assigning them to an interface, regardless of whether they are obtained through stateless autoconfiguration, DHCPv6, or manual configuration.

sources.title: RFC 4443 — Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMPv6) for IPv6 [Conta et al., 2006]
sources.verifying_quote: ICMPv6 is used by IPv6 nodes to report errors encountered in processing packets, and to perform other internet-layer functions, such as diagnostics. ICMPv6 is an integral part of IPv6, and the base protocol MUST be fully implemented by every IPv6 node.

sources.title: Internal KB D4 — ARP and Neighbor Discovery (kb/networking/ip-addressing/arp-and-ndp.json)
sources.verifying_quote: IPv6 Neighbor Solicitation (NS) replaces ARP, sent to solicited-node multicast address (ff02::1:ff00:0/104 + last 24 bits of target IP). Neighbor cache maintains state: INCOMPLETE, REACHABLE, STALE, DELAY, PROBE.
